front: Poem: villanelle addressed to the speaker's dying father, with refrain 'Rage, rage against the dying of the light'
back: Do not go gentle into that good night (Dylan Thomas)
work: Do not go gentle into that good night

front: Poem: categorizes wise men, good men, wild men, and grave men, each resisting death differently
back: Do not go gentle into that good night (Dylan Thomas)
work: Do not go gentle into that good night

front: Poem: 'Blind eyes could blaze like meteors and be gay' — reference to the speaker's father's blindness
back: Do not go gentle into that good night (Dylan Thomas)
work: Do not go gentle into that good night

front: Poem: speaker asks father to 'Curse, bless, me now with your fierce tears' at the end of this villanelle
back: Do not go gentle into that good night (Dylan Thomas)
work: Do not go gentle into that good night

front: Poem: ends 'Time held me green and dying / Though I sang in my chains like the sea'
Poem: speaker recalls being 'young and easy under the apple boughs' at his aunt's farm
Poem: speaker is 'honoured among the foxes and pheasants' and 'famous among the barns'
Poem: stanzas each end with a statement beginning 'And I am dumb to tell...'; from the collection 18 Poems
back: The force that through the green fuse drives the flower (Dylan Thomas)
work: The force that through the green fuse drives the flower

front: Poem: 'of my clay is made the hangman's lime' — the force driving the speaker's 'green age' is also his destroyer
back: The force that through the green fuse drives the flower (Dylan Thomas)
work: The force that through the green fuse drives the flower

front: Poem: title from Paul's epistle to the Romans; title line opens and closes all three stanzas; declares 'Though lovers be lost love shall not'
back: And death shall have no dominion (Dylan Thomas)
work: And death shall have no dominion

front: Poem: concludes 'after the first death, there is no other'; about a child killed in the London Blitz
back: A Refusal to Mourn the Death, by Fire, of a Child in London (Dylan Thomas)
work: A Refusal to Mourn the Death, by Fire, of a Child in London

front: Poem: the dead are 'robed in the long friends, the grains beyond age, the dark veins of her mother'
back: A Refusal to Mourn the Death, by Fire, of a Child in London (Dylan Thomas)
work: A Refusal to Mourn the Death, by Fire, of a Child in London

front: Play: radio 'play for voices' set in the fictional seaside town of Llareggub ('bugger all' reversed); features Captain Cat and Mrs. Ogmore-Pritchard
Author: his father, an English teacher in Wales described as 'a cold kind man brave in his narrow pride,' inspired both 'Do not go gentle' and the unfinished poem 'Elegy'
Author: Roald Dahl built a replica of this poet's writing shed
Author: 'Poem in October' marks his 'thirtieth year to heaven'
